operating_state: Total revenue increased 2% to $53.6 billion driven by 23 net new warehouses opened since prior year, while comparable sales remained flat due to foreign exchange and gasoline price headwinds. Excluding these impacts, comparable sales grew 3% with increased shopping frequency offset by lower average ticket. E-commerce declined 10% reflecting normalization from pandemic-elevated levels. Membership fee revenue increased 6% driven by new sign-ups, Executive upgrades, and renewal rates reaching 92.6% in US/Canada.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage improved 13 basis points to 10.32% driven by core merchandise categories and absence of prior year LIFO charge, though excluding gasoline deflation impact decreased 3 basis points with increased rewards negatively impacting margins. SG&A expenses as percentage of sales increased 49 basis points to 9.11% due to wage increases implemented across multiple periods, combined with slower sales growth reducing expense leverage. Operating income declined with discontinued charter shipping activities charge.
material_events: Board declared quarterly dividend increase to $1.02 per share up from $0.90 in prior year. Share repurchases totaled $447 million under new authorization with capacity remaining through January 2027. Company recognized asset impairments primarily related to discontinued charter shipping activities. Opened 17 new warehouses including relocations with additional locations planned for remainder of fiscal year.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency headwinds negatively impacted net sales by 152 basis points with gasoline price deflation reducing sales by 167 basis points though volume increased. Interest rate environment provided benefit with interest income increasing significantly from prior year quarter. Merchandise costs continue impacted by inflation with company employing supplier cost-sharing, volume purchasing, and selective price increases to manage pressures.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures financed through operating cash flows and existing liquidity. Company anticipates ongoing square footage growth particularly in international markets, though available desirable sites becoming more difficult to secure in mature markets. E-commerce operations expected to continue normalization while maintaining strategic importance for member engagement.
uncertainty_profile: Company faces ongoing legal proceedings including California labor law claims, wage and hour class actions, and opioid-related litigation, with management believing pending litigation will not have material adverse effect though unfavorable outcomes could result in charges material to individual quarters. Construction and purchase obligations primarily due within 12 months in normal course of business.
